title: betula alba f. verrucosa
intro: Betula alba f. verrucosa, also known as the white birch, is a deciduous tree species with distinctive white, papery bark and serrated leaves. The medicinal properties of Betula alba f. verrucosa include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include reducing fever, relieving pain, and soothing digestive issues due to its salicin content. Herbal preparations of Betula alba f. verrucosa can be found in the form of tea, tincture, and salves, which are often used to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne.
